items.title: OpenAI claims a major math proof
items.oneliner: OpenAI says GPT-5.6 proved Cycle Double Cover. If it survives review, formal reasoning just got very real.

items.title: Xiaomi details long-context inference economics
items.oneliner: Xiaomi says Hybrid SWA cuts KV cache to one-seventh. Long-context serving is becoming a systems problem, not magic.

items.title: Ultrasound silent speech gets usable accuracy
items.oneliner: Aleph Neuro hit 15.6% word error from tongue ultrasound with 50 hours of data. Private voice interfaces look plausible.

items.title: Apple sues OpenAI over trade secrets
items.oneliner: Apple sued OpenAI over alleged trade secret theft by ex-employees. Frontier hiring now carries serious IP blast radius.

items.title: Residential proxy scraping keeps escalating
items.oneliner: Residential proxy scraping keeps mutating after botnet takedowns. Public web defenses are losing against AI-scale extraction.

items.title: AI surveillance is becoming operational
items.oneliner: Schneier warns AI surveillance is moving from theory to enforcement. Product teams should expect regulation to follow deployment.

items.title: A library update crushed MPI bandwidth
items.oneliner: Guix traced Open MPI bandwidth collapsing from 25 GB/s to 2.3 GB/s. Dependency drift can silently destroy infrastructure performance.

items.title: C++ to safe Rust gets practical
items.oneliner: Cpp2Rust promises automatic translation from C++ into safe Rust. Legacy code migration may finally get a credible accelerator.

items.title: Scarf moved off Haskell after seven years
items.oneliner: Scarf left Haskell after seven years, citing compile times and ecosystem pain. AI coding shifts language tradeoffs toward pragmatism.

items.title: America still cannot make medical gloves
items.oneliner: Nearly $1 billion later, the US still cannot make medical gloves domestically. Supply chain resilience remains mostly PowerPoint.
